evidence.organoid_type: Healthy tissue derived organoids were cultured in Human Intestinal Stem Cell medium (HISC).
evidence.source_cells.cell_type: From the resected colon segment, normal as well as tumor tissue was isolated. The isolation of healthy crypts and tumor epithelium was performed essentially as described by Sato et al.
evidence.source_cells.species: Human Tissues Colonic tissues were obtained from The Diakonessen Hospital Utrecht with informed consent
evidence.matrix: 8μl of ~7mg/ml BME was dispensed in to 384-well microplates and allowed to polymerize.
evidence.base_media: The composition of HISC is: Basal culture medium with 50% Wnt conditioned medium
evidence.Wnt conditioned medium: Basal culture medium with 50% Wnt conditioned medium

notes: Source cells are human adult colonic primary tissue (healthy crypts and tumor epithelium) from colorectal cancer patients; isolation was delegated to Sato et al. 2011 ('performed essentially as described'), but the full HISC media composition (reagents + concentrations) IS reported in THIS paper's methods_text, so signaling factors and supplements are taken directly from the source, not imported. cell_type coded as primary_tissue (patient-derived colonic crypts/tumor epithelium); could also be viewed as adult_stem_cell (Lgr5 crypt stem cells) but the methods text states tissue/crypt isolation, so primary_tissue is the conservative choice -- FLAG for Rye. base_media: HISC is built on 'Basal culture medium' which is named but its identity/formulation is NOT specified in methods_text (no Advanced DMEM/F12, etc. given), so value=null/not_reported. matrix: the methods state organoids were grown in BME (Basement Membrane Extract) -- BME is named explicitly only in the 'Organoid viability assays' section ('~7mg/ml BME') and figure captions, not in the main 'Organoid culture' paragraph, which does not name the embedding matrix; the Introduction (body_text, not methods_text) mentions 'Basement Membrane Extracts (BME)'. Coded reported with the verbatim viability-assay quote -- FLAG that matrix is inferred from the assay context, not the primary culture paragraph. Tumor organoids used 'HICS minus Wnt' (Wnt-withdrawn), i.e. Wnt is omitted for tumor organoids; annotation reflects the healthy/complete HISC formulation. Concentration unit for SB202190 kept as 'uM' verbatim (paper writes '3 uM'); n-Acetyl Cysteine '1,25 mM' uses a comma decimal (=1.25 mM) but is a supplement so no concentration field. Gastrin and Prostaglandine E2 roles left null (not described in text). Nicotinamide and n-Acetyl Cysteine classified as supplements per annotation rules.
